claim: Continuous narcissism trait masks discrete subgroups differing in motivational pathways and adjustment profiles.

phenomenon: emotional processing deficit rescued by matching context
claim: Contextual emotional cues modulate expression of underlying emotional processing deficit.

phenomenon: perception shapes outcome more than reality
claim: Optimistic perception of relational resources mediates stress-buffering effect more than actual resource provision.
Informal competitive pressure directs formal firms toward differentiation innovation when formal rivals and informal-adaptation alternatives are scarce.

phenomenon: framing flips behavioral type distribution by gender
claim: Taking frame makes males selfish and females egalitarian; giving frame inverts pattern, nullifying aggregate effect.

phenomenon: asymmetry in access creates fairness-contingent investment cycle
claim: Downstream participants condition future infrastructure investment on fair resource distribution, creating feedback loop that prevents under-provision despite sequential access disadvantage.
